steps.title: Find the contextual signal
steps.content: The corpus is used to test hypotheses about how often a word appears — the example is "world" being the eighth most commonly used noun. The blank must mean common or frequent.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean how widespread or frequently occurring a word is in English. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — pervasive: "how pervasive a word is" — pervasive means widespread and frequently occurring throughout. This matches the frequency concept. ✓
  • Choice B — credible: "how credible a word is" — credible means believable or trustworthy. Words are not evaluated for trustworthiness here. ✗
  • Choice C — assertive: "how assertive a word is" — assertive means confident or forceful in manner. This describes attitude, not frequency. ✗
  • Choice D — profound: "how profound a word is" — profound means having deep meaning. The passage is about occurrence count, not depth of meaning. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
